title: It Gets Better

jokes.text: Carlos was just... tickling me.
jokes.setup: Narrator says George Michael is surrounded by people thrilled to be in his company
jokes.punchline: George Michael's uncomfortable explanation suggests inappropriate workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Immediately establishes the awkward dynamic and George Michael's discomfort with his newfound popularity

jokes.text: You're dead, man, you are so dead. That was the last root beer snow cone.
jokes.setup: Employees fighting over who gets George Michael things
jokes.punchline: Death threats over a root beer snow cone reveals the absurd lengths they'll go to
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how desperate the employees are to please George Michael

jokes.text: You know my name! What's his name? You're the kiss-ass, Carlos. What's his name?
jokes.setup: Carlos calls someone else a kiss-ass
jokes.punchline: George Michael immediately proves Carlos is the real kiss-ass by knowing his name
jokes.explanation: Smart reversal that exposes the hypocrisy while showing George Michael's awareness

jokes.text: I barely know their names. I mean, I know Carlos, but... only because of that old song 'Carlos the Tickle Monster.'
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to downplay his connection to the employees
jokes.punchline: Makes up an obviously fake song to explain remembering Carlos's name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist invention that callbacks to earlier awkwardness while showing George Michael's desperate improvisation

jokes.text: Wait, what song is that? Oh, god, I knew he made that up.
jokes.setup: George Michael's obviously fake song reference
jokes.punchline: Rebel immediately calls him out and he's caught
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing on the callback payoff showing George Michael's lie immediately exposed

jokes.text: And... that's where the song should've ended. So I guess we all got a little carried away. Are you sure? We all ended at the same time.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to be diplomatic about the band's performance
jokes.punchline: His attempt at politeness is immediately contradicted by the band
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's conflict avoidance personality clashing with his artistic ambitions

jokes.text: George Michael's wood block band ending at the same time because 'you all sped up together' - his superiority about his 'amazing internal clock'
jokes.setup: George Michael critiques his band's timing
jokes.punchline: He pompously credits his 'amazing internal clock' while being completely wrong about the musical issue
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing George Michael's misplaced confidence and musical pretensions.

jokes.text: But this is about my demo tape to Juilliard and they've got to dig what I do so much that they start an entire department for wood block.
jokes.setup: George Michael taking his music seriously
jokes.punchline: Believes Juilliard will create a whole department for wood block
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George Michael's delusional confidence about his niche talent - genuinely absurd but character-specific

jokes.text: I mean, the whole point of the band is to showcase my amazing internal clock. You know, that's something you have to be born with. Or bred with.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining his musical talent
jokes.punchline: The transition from 'born with' to 'bred with' suggests genetic manipulation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the BabyTock callback perfectly while showing George Michael's pretentiousness

jokes.text: BabyTock promotional video where George Michael appears as a test baby
jokes.setup: Flashback to George Michael's childhood
jokes.punchline: He was used as a test subject for a dubious baby development product made from failed Cornballers
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes George Michael's 'internal clock' as product of questionable family business practices.

jokes.text: And if I were a doctor, I would guarantee you that it would also boost their brain power 110%.
jokes.setup: Promotional video for baby development product
jokes.punchline: Disclaims not being a doctor while making medical claims with impossible percentages
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development absurdist logic with perfect character-specific dishonesty

jokes.text: That's a lot of brain power.
jokes.setup: 110% brain power claim
jokes.punchline: Interviewer's deadpan response to the impossible statistic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan response that highlights the absurdity

jokes.text: Uh, well, that's impossible... To deny.
jokes.setup: Asked if the product changed her baby's brain power
jokes.punchline: Starts to say impossible but Lucille redirects to make it positive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with perfect economy - Lucille's manipulation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: So we're stuck with 5,000 of these worthless corn-ballers?
jokes.setup: Discussion of failed product
jokes.punchline: References the infamous Cornballer from earlier seasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that rewards longtime viewers while setting up the BabyTock origin story

jokes.text: Perfect for those who like the sound of a clock, but don't know their numbers.
jokes.setup: Describing the timer without numbers
jokes.punchline: Creates an absurd target demographic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic that perfectly captures the show's surreal business thinking

jokes.text: Mother of (bleep)! We should put these in cribs.
jokes.setup: Cornballer presumably burns George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Despite being burned, immediately suggests putting dangerous product in baby cribs
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - George Sr.'s complete disregard for safety while being actively injured by the product

jokes.text: That's not a half puny thought.
jokes.setup: Trying to say 'half bad'
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'half bad' with 'puny' creating nonsense
jokes.explanation: Decent malapropism showing George Sr.'s verbal incompetence

jokes.text: Hitting a block of wood.
jokes.setup: George Michael says he wants to pursue music
jokes.punchline: P-Hound reduces it to its most basic description
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of George Michael's pretensions with brutal economy

jokes.text: Before the world cracked me open and I became the guy that you're looking at now. George Michael 2.0. God, I hate my name.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to sound worldly and changed
jokes.punchline: The dramatic language undercut by complaining about his ordinary name
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George Michael's attempt at sophistication undermined by basic insecurities

jokes.text: Rip it up... right down the center. That's it. Rip it. Couple more. Did you get it? Proud of you.
jokes.setup: Michael angrily giving George Michael money then taking it back
jokes.punchline: Methodically instructing the destruction while maintaining encouraging tone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly passive-aggressive moment that shows Michael's petty cruelty disguised as guidance

jokes.text: N... no. No, no. (laughs): What?
jokes.setup: George Michael arriving at college in a damaged truck
jokes.punchline: His incredulous reaction to his situation
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of George Michael's college arrival gone wrong

jokes.text: Let's make it a... Pepsi. Drinking Pepsi in the morning.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to be rebellious
jokes.punchline: His idea of breaking taboos is drinking Pepsi at breakfast
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's hilariously innocent view of rebellion

jokes.text: Oh, excuse me, I forgot it wasn't morning. (laughing): I forgot it wasn't morning.
jokes.setup: George Michael ordering eggs at night
jokes.punchline: His awkward explanation becomes a running bit with others laughing
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael accidentally finding social success through awkwardness

jokes.text: And that's what we call only being behind by three.
jokes.setup: Describing a softball game
jokes.punchline: Trying to make being behind sound positive
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific optimistic spin on losing

jokes.text: You guys broke up. Oh, thanks, Ray, I almost forgot.
jokes.setup: George Michael apparently telling a story about Becky
jokes.punchline: Ray's blunt reminder that they broke up
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan interruption that deflates George Michael's reminiscing

jokes.text: Is that... ? Okay, I guess it's not, uh, not gonna be a person.
jokes.setup: George Michael volunteering for kissing experiment
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's going to kiss a machine, not a person
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing George Michael's desperation for income leading to awkward situation

jokes.text: And C? I think one on C. M? M? Oh, one on... M? One for sure on... one. And Z? One.
jokes.setup: Rating pleasure levels during kissing experiment
jokes.punchline: Every single rating is the lowest possible score
jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe comedy showing George Michael's complete failure at the experiment

jokes.text: You make love like a boy. Spanish, speak to me in Spanish. Because the only way that college credit is actually activated is if I have...
jokes.setup: Sexual encounter abroad
jokes.punchline: George Michael's academic concerns during intimate moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing George Michael's priorities even during supposedly transformative sexual experience

jokes.text: Dominarme. Cook... cook at... Cooking at you? Overpower me. Oh, yes, make me cry. You're a terrible mother.
jokes.setup: Passionate Spanish dialogue during sex
jokes.punchline: George Michael's translation attempt leads to insulting her parenting
jokes.explanation: Absolute comedy gold - the misdirection from passionate Spanish to parenting insult is perfectly executed and uniquely George Michael

jokes.text: It gets better.
jokes.setup: George Michael returning to campus with new confidence
jokes.punchline: The episode title delivered as his new catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Meta moment that titles the episode while showing George Michael's newfound confidence

jokes.text: I sort of feel like a king returning to his castle. Only to discover that they thought he was two kings. Double castle.
jokes.setup: George Michael's royal metaphor for returning to school
jokes.punchline: Mix-up leads to doubling everything including made-up 'double castle'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mistaken identity premise with absurd logic

jokes.text: Can't wait to meet Michael, George.
jokes.setup: They think George Michael is two different people
jokes.punchline: Eagerly anticipating meeting 'Michael' who is standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the mistaken identity running gag

jokes.text: Otherwise, they're gonna think you're a musician, huh?
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on George Michael's mustache
jokes.punchline: Treats being seen as a musician as something to avoid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment showing his dismissiveness toward artistic pursuits

jokes.text: That sounds like a mother of an invention.
jokes.setup: P-Hound mentions electric wood block
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'mother of invention' phrase
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that sets up the app development storyline

jokes.text: Well, these are a (bleep) joke. Where are the mallet options? There's no choice of wood weight or grain type. There's no diameter hole option. I mean, what is this, BabyTock?
jokes.setup: Examining competitor wood block apps
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific complaints culminating in BabyTock reference
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing George Michael's obsessive perfectionism with clever callback

jokes.text: You know, the sharp metal box that you put in the crib. Is that not a well-known... ?
jokes.setup: Describing BabyTock casually
jokes.punchline: Assumes everyone knows about the dangerous baby product
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy callback highlighting the absurd danger of the family product

jokes.text: Block-block? No. People are going to think we're in the chicken-noise business. How is that a chicken noise? You know... well, it's kind of hard to do in these matador pants, but, you know... Block-bl...
jokes.setup: Naming the app
jokes.punchline: George Michael's attempt to demonstrate chicken noise while wearing matador pants
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with physical comedy element and random matador pants detail

jokes.text: It's $5,000 for the domain name, and then there's this whole XML Web site fee. I got the last one. That was 99 cents. Times three.
jokes.setup: Domain name costs vs app costs
jokes.punchline: Massive domain fee contrasted with tiny app purchases
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing the real cost of business vs research expenses

jokes.text: Oh, it's her last name. But her most recent grade is... well, this just shows an Elvis Presley head.
jokes.setup: Asking about the 'F' in Maeby's name
jokes.punchline: Her grade is represented by Elvis imagery instead of letters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag showing Maeby's academic performance through absurd imagery

jokes.text: So, that bad? I'll take her. Stay on top of her. You may need to ride her pretty hard.
jokes.setup: Discussing tutoring Maeby
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo in educational context
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo humor that's functional but relies on cheap double entendre

jokes.text: Was she your first? Cousin? No. Why would you... why would your mind go right to... ? No.
jokes.setup: P-Hound asking if Maeby was George Michael's first
jokes.punchline: George Michael assumes he means cousin, revealing the family secret
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that exposes the cousin relationship while showing George Michael's defensive guilt

jokes.text: Overtly sexual. Right. As I live and breathe. Crazy.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining his new persona
jokes.punchline: Uses old-fashioned expression while claiming to be overtly sexual
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing George Michael's pretensions vs reality

jokes.text: But for the phrase, 'as I live and breathe,' he nailed it.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to act surprised to see Maeby
jokes.punchline: Narrator points out the one phrase that ruined his performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator intervention highlighting George Michael's one mistake in his deception

jokes.text: Well, don't forget... I'm a Harris. Harris? Yeah. Someone who 'inharris' a lot of money.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining her financial situation
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'inharris' as verb meaning inherits
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Maeby's casual attitude toward wealth and language

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you know, I wouldn't put all my Anns in that basket.
jokes.setup: Responding to Maeby's inheritance comment
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'eggs' with 'Anns' referencing his ex-girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly incorporates character history into common expression

jokes.text: You've never been a risk taker. I like to (bleep) with people. That's what I do. I'm making a statement.
jokes.setup: Maeby describing her philosophy
jokes.punchline: Crude description of her motivation for staying in high school
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Maeby's rebellious but ultimately aimless nature

jokes.text: I made a 40-year-old woman cry in Spain.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to prove his sexual prowess
jokes.punchline: The claim could be interpreted multiple ways beyond sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and ambiguity - could reference his sexual prowess or the 'terrible mother' insult

jokes.text: Well, you know, in algebra, you have an unknown element, and, uh, we call it 'X,' and then you have to solve for it to find out what number 'X' is. 'X' isn't a number.
jokes.setup: George Michael's attempt at sexy algebra metaphor
jokes.punchline: Maeby's basic correction ruins his seduction attempt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of seduction attempt through George Michael's need to educate despite being corrected

jokes.text: But in that moment, the urge to educate her was more powerful than the urge to kiss her.
jokes.setup: George Michael torn between seduction and teaching
jokes.punchline: His academic instincts overpower his sexual ones
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight showing George Michael's fundamental nature overriding his attempts at change

jokes.text: Yeah, let's get wasted. Let's drink that formula. It's not that kind of formula.
jokes.setup: George Michael's romantic formula metaphor
jokes.punchline: Maeby thinks he's talking about alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing communication breakdown between the cousins

jokes.text: He had not responded now for 41 seconds, according to his unfailing internal clock, and it was time to come clean.
jokes.setup: George Michael's long pause before lying
jokes.punchline: His precise timing ability from BabyTock being used for lying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that ties his childhood conditioning to adult deception

jokes.text: And he found himself suddenly and effortlessly tapping into a long-inbred instinct for lying. A Bluth taking his first steps in deceit.
jokes.setup: George Michael starting to lie about Fakeblock
jokes.punchline: Narrator frames it as genetic destiny and family tradition
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment showing George Michael's inheritance of family traits

jokes.text: Ay, dios mio.
jokes.setup: George Michael seeing embarrassing photo of himself online
jokes.punchline: Uses Spanish expression from his transformation period
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how his Spanish experience affected his expressions

jokes.text: No, he wouldn't. He... I... I was just leaning back, so it looked like I... I got this chair again, didn't I?
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to explain why his father would vote against himself
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by chair malfunction mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy interruption of George Michael's scheming

jokes.text: That was fun. Did he look betrayed to you? He kind of always looks that way.
jokes.setup: Asking about Michael's reaction to being voted out
jokes.punchline: P-Hound's observation about Michael's perpetual expression
jokes.explanation: Good observational joke about Michael's character

jokes.text: Well, I'm getting a lifetime achievement award at this gala. Well, you might want to get that out of the way, too, I guess.
jokes.setup: Maeby mentioning lifetime achievement award
jokes.punchline: George Michael's casual dismissal of major honor as something to 'get out of the way'
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan moment showing George Michael's academic priorities over major achievements

jokes.text: Because I can't bring any of my friends without getting permission slips from their parents.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining why she wants George Michael as her date
jokes.punchline: Reveals the real issue with dating people her apparent age
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail showing the practical problems of Maeby's high school charade

jokes.text: Oh, he's nobody. Oh. It's Justin. That's David Henrie. He's Justin from Wizards of Waverly Place. Hmm. It's on a lot in Spain.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to seem unimpressed by celebrity
jokes.punchline: Has to explain how he knows the show through his Spanish experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's pretensions undermined by his actual knowledge

jokes.text: Buenas noches, pequeño Scarlet. I will be needing a room, please. How many nights? Two. Sorry. No, just one night. I thought you were saying how many people.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to be suave at hotel
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets confused about basic room booking questions
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing George Michael's attempted sophistication immediately falling apart

jokes.text: One bed, one night, two people, let's solve for 'X.' I hate young Hollywood.
jokes.setup: George Michael using his algebra seduction line
jokes.punchline: Hotel clerk's disgusted dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff showing how his line sounds to others

jokes.text: So funny that didn't come up in the car.
jokes.setup: Learning Maeby has a boyfriend at the event
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to major romantic obstacle
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction showing George Michael's deflation

jokes.text: That geek Maharis destroyed the whole thing. And you know why? Just because he could. Those Internet geniuses... that's what they do. I'm ruined. I'll probably never get to drive the Schnoodle car.
jokes.setup: George Michael being credited with cyber attack
jokes.punchline: The executive's loss of the 'Schnoodle car' as his main concern
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist priority - company car over company destruction

jokes.text: You know, it's like the time that you told that girl that you were George Takei's son. Well, it's too late. This thing's taking off.
jokes.setup: George Michael comparing lies
jokes.punchline: Reference to P-Hound's own celebrity lie and the situation spiraling
jokes.explanation: Decent comparison showing both characters lie about identity

jokes.text: Well, you may have put in the most money to Fakeblock, but I put in the first three dollars, so that makes you a hop-on. No, you're the hop-on, Paul, and I know exactly who you're trying to hop on to. Maeby. Maeby.
jokes.setup: Argument over Fakeblock ownership
jokes.punchline: Escalating 'hop-on' accusations ending in revealing Maeby as the target
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using established Arrested Development terminology

jokes.text: I trust you, George Michael. I would not get into bed with you on this thing if I didn't trust you. No. Yeah, of... of course you can trust me in bed.
jokes.setup: Maeby talking about business partnership
jokes.punchline: George Michael interprets everything sexually
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo humor showing George Michael's one-track mind

jokes.text: We'd just lie there? I mean, I'm talking about business. So am I. That's why we would just lie there.
jokes.setup: Maeby confused by George Michael's bed comment
jokes.punchline: George Michael doubles down on the lying/lying pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on lying (deception) vs lying (reclining) that perfectly captures the show's wit

jokes.text: when you become an Internet billionaire, you could do whatever you want with me in bed. It was that. That's what made him change his mind.
jokes.setup: Various factors pushing George Michael toward continuing the lie
jokes.punchline: Narrator identifies the exact sexual motivation that tips the scales
jokes.explanation: Good narrator intervention pinpointing George Michael's obvious motivation

jokes.text: That was literally the first thing I asked you. So you're giving me these crazy... Mad?
jokes.setup: George Michael frustrated with Gob's unclear communication
jokes.punchline: Gob's completely unhelpful single-word response
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Gob's typical unhelpfulness

jokes.text: It's my stupid brother! He looks just like me. I'll just go up, I'll find him, I'll... Oh, come on.
jokes.setup: Andy Richter being denied entry to club
jokes.punchline: Claims his brother is already inside and looks identical
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Andy Richter's real-life identical brothers

jokes.text: Well, you know, I, uh... I prefer a woman who aims higher.
jokes.setup: Rebel saying women throw themselves at his feet
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre about anatomy
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted innuendo that shows George Michael successfully being suave for once

jokes.text: Just remind me not to go in the men's room!
jokes.setup: Page for 'George Michael' over loudspeaker
jokes.punchline: Rebel's assumption about what that name means in a men's room
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo about the singer George Michael, relies on cheap reference

jokes.text: Bet you 500 bucks they find a dead kid behind the stick. You know, or maybe on the dead dad's lap whose only fault was loving that kid too much and letting him fly the plane.
jokes.setup: Michael speculating about plane crash
jokes.punchline: Increasingly morbid and specific details about dead family
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Michael's morbid imagination while avoiding his son

jokes.text: Well, you know, a month ago, I would have told you that he and I were exactly the same, that we were twins. But now, I wouldn't even think of my son as my brother.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his relationship with George Michael
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes incestuous family dynamic while meaning to show distance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable line that accidentally reveals the show's recurring incest themes

jokes.text: It was 99 cents. Times three. Times three.
jokes.setup: Lawsuit over Fakeblock ownership
jokes.punchline: Repeated emphasis on the trivial amount of money
jokes.explanation: Good running gag payoff emphasizing the absurd scale of the legal battle

jokes.text: Take to the sea!
jokes.setup: Legal advice to George Michael
jokes.punchline: Classic Arrested Development legal advice to flee by boat
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to one of the show's most beloved running gags with maximum economy

tmdb_overview: At UC Irvine things get steamy when George-Michael finds himself in a love triangle with his best friend Ray and his girlfriend Becky.
insight: Season finale threads together 75 callbacks as the family's schemes finally collapse spectacularly.
